[文献] Methods: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial at 12 centers across China (January-June 2023). 4,872 participants aged 65-85 years with prior inactivated COVID-19 vaccination we
Characteristic Vaccine (N=2,436) Placebo (N=2,436)
Age (years), mean 72.4 72.1
Female, n (%) 1,267 (52.0) 1,243 (51.0)
Prior CoronaVac, n (%) 1,462 (60.0) 1,474 (60.5)
ABSTRACT
Background: The Omicron BA.5 subvariant demonstrates significant immune evasion. The effectiveness of mRNA-1273 bivalent booster in preventing symptomatic infection among elderly adults (greater than or equal to 65 years) in China remains uncertain. Methods: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial at 12 centers across China (January-June 2023). 4,872 participants aged 65-85 years with prior inactivated COVID-19 vaccination were randomized 1:1 to mRNA-1273 bivalent booster (50 micrograms) or placebo. Primary endpoint: symptomatic COVID-19 at least 14 days post-booster. Secondary endpoints: severe COVID-19, neutralizing antibody titers, and adverse events. Results: Symptomatic COVID-19: 23/2,418 (0.95%) vaccine vs 89/2,403 (3.70%) placebo (efficacy 74.3%; 95% CI 62.1-82.8; P less than 0.001). Severe COVID-19: 1 (0.04%) vs 11 (0.46%) (efficacy 90.9%; 95% CI 58.3-98.9). GMT against BA.5 increased 30.9-fold. Common AEs: injection-site pain (58.3%), fatigue (32.1%), headache (24.7%).
[实体] Vaccine, Placebo, Age, Female, Prior CoronaVac

text:
[文献] Methods: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial at 12 centers across China (January-June 2023). 4,872 participants aged 65-85 years with prior inactivated COVID-19 vaccination we
Grade 3+ AEs: 2.8% vs 2.1%
Conclusions: mRNA-1273 BA.5 bivalent booster showed 74.3% efficacy against symptomatic COVID-19 and 90.9% against severe disease in elderly Chinese adults with acceptable safety. These findings support mRNA boosters for elderly populations. METHODS
Study Design: Investigator-initiated, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial (NCT05891234). Approved by IRB at each center. Independent DSMB reviewed safety data. Participants: Adults 65-85 years, two prior doses of inactivated COVID-19 vaccine (CoronaVac or BBIBP-CorV) at least 6 months prior. Exclusions: SARS-CoV-2 infection within 90 days, prior COVID-19 booster, immunosuppressive therapy, known vaccine allergy. Endpoints: Primary: symptomatic COVID-19 (RT-PCR confirmed) at least 14 days post-booster. Secondary: severe COVID-19, GMT against BA.5 at day 28, solicited AEs (7 days), unsolicited AEs (28 days), SAEs throughout study. Table 1. Baseline Characteristics
Chen L, Wang M, Zhang H, et al. | N Engl J Med 2023;389:2134-2144

Table 2. Primary and Key Secondary Endpoints
Endpoint Vaccine (N=2,418) Placebo (N=2,403) Efficacy (95% CI)
Symptomatic COVID-19 23 (0.95%) 89 (3.70%) 74.3% (62.1-82.8)
Severe COVID-19 1 (0.04%) 11 (0.46%) 90.9% (58.3-98.9)
COVID-19 hospitalization 2 (0.08%) 14 (0.58%) 85.7% (55.2-96.1)
Any COVID-19 related death 0 3 (0.12%) 100% (--) 
DISCUSSION
This trial demonstrates that an mRNA-1273 BA.5-adapted bivalent booster provides substantial protection (74.3% efficacy) against symptomatic COVID-19 in elderly Chinese adults. The 90.9% efficacy against severe disease is particularly important given the high risk in this age group. The 30.9-fold increase in neutralizing antibodies corroborates clinical efficacy. The safety profile was consistent with prior mRNA vaccine studies. Subgroup analysis showed similar efficacy across age strata (65-74 vs 75-85 years). Limitations include short follow-up (median 4.2 months) and inability to assess asymptomatic infection. Longer follow-up is ongoing.
